summary:
The loss of a permanent first molar leads to increased spacing which causes the neighboring second molar to shift towards the open area, typically moving buccally due to balancing dental pressures. Agent1 reasoned based on typical orthodontic principles where missing molars create spaces prompting other teeth like the second molar to migrate into these areas. \boxed{\text{The second molar moves buccally}} To keep it concise while capturing essential details, I refined this summary focusing directly on what happens with specific emphasis on directionality as per common outcomes observed clinically when such scenarios occur. 

To ensure clarity without extraneous detail but maintaining core elements related specifically addressing movement post-lost primary/secondary molar contextually within human dentition patterns.
